description: Console resolution mode (max, keep, or specific like 1920x1080)
name: myModules.boot.boot.consoleMode

description: Whether to enable Boot configuration.
name: myModules.boot.boot.enable

description: Systemd initrd for Plymouth
name: myModules.boot.boot.initrd.enable

description: Bootloader to use
name: myModules.boot.boot.loader

description: Whether to enable Plymouth boot splash.
name: myModules.boot.boot.plymouth.enable

description: Plymouth theme to use
name: myModules.boot.boot.plymouth.theme

description: Whether to enable Lanzaboote secure boot.
name: myModules.boot.boot.secureBoot.enable

description: Path to PKI bundle
name: myModules.boot.boot.secureBoot.pkiBundle

description: Whether to enable Hibernate support via a LUKS-encrypted swap partition.
name: myModules.boot.hibernate.enable

description:
Physical RAM size in GiB. Hibernate requires swap partition
to be at least this large (the full RAM image is written to
swap). Asserted at eval time as a sanity check.
name: myModules.boot.hibernate.ramSizeGB

description:
Priority for the disk swap device. Default 10 keeps it above
disabled priorities but below typical zram priority (100) so
compressed RAM is preferred under steady-state memory
pressure. Disk swap engages primarily for hibernate images.
name: myModules.boot.hibernate.swapDevicePriority

description:
UUID of the LUKS container holding swap. Get it with
`sudo cryptsetup luksUUID /dev/<swap-partition>`.
name: myModules.boot.hibernate.swapLuksUuid

description: Whether to enable Impermanence (erase root on every boot).
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.enable

description: Extra system directories to persist
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.extraDirectories
type: list of (string or attribute set of anything)

description: Extra system files to persist
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.extraFiles

description: LUKS device mapper name (e.g. cryptroot)
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.luksDevice

description: Mountpoint for the persistent BTRFS subvolume
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.persistPath

description: Name of the read-only blank root snapshot
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.rollback.blankSnapshot

description: Enable initrd rollback service that erases root on every boot
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.rollback.enable

description: Name of the root BTRFS subvolume
name: myModules.boot.impermanence.rollback.rootSubvolume

description: Enable BBR3 TCP congestion control.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.bbr3

description: Extra -O3 / compiler aggressiveness.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.ccHarder

description: CPU scheduler flavor the CachyOS kernel is built with.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.cpusched

description: Transparent Hugepage default.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.hugepage

description: Timer frequency (HZ).
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.hzTicks

description: Kernel Control Flow Integrity.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.kcfi

description: Default to the `performance` cpufreq governor at boot.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.performanceGovernor

description: Kernel preemption model.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.preemptType

description: Tickless mode.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.cachyos.tickrate

description:
CachyOS channel selector (ignored for non-CachyOS variants).
`rc` disables LTO + per-arch variants.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.channel

description: Whether to enable custom kernel variant selection.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.enable

description: Extra kernel cmdline parameters to append.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.extraParams

description:
Microarchitecture for CachyOS kernel variants. Ignored for stock
variants. Use `none` to get the CachyOS generic build.

When left as `null`, defaults to the `x86-64-v{N}` generic build
matching `myModules.host.tier` (v2 → x86-64-v2, etc.). Hosts
that want a uarch-specific compile target (e.g. `ZEN5` on Zen 5
hardware) set this explicitly.
name: myModules.boot.kernel.mArch

description:
Kernel variant. Stock nixpkgs variants are resolved from a lookup
table; CachyOS variants go through the CachyOS override path and
pick up `channel` + `mArch` + `cachyos.*` sub-options.

**Ivy Bridge (x86-64-v2) compatibility**: CachyOS kernels
default to x86-64-v3 (AVX2 required) since the 2025 v3 bump.
Setting `mArch = "x86-64-v2"` routes to the v2-compiled
variant. Upstream `cachyos-settings-nix` publishes both
`linux-cachyos-lts-lto-x86_64-v2` and `linux-cachyos-latest-
lto-x86_64-v2` — both Ivy-Bridge safe. `cachyos-rc` has no
per-arch variants (channel=rc short-circuits the suffix).
name: myModules.boot.kernel.variant

description: Whether to enable Graphics support.
name: myModules.hardware.graphics.enable

name: myModules.hardware.graphics.enable32Bit

description:
GPU vendors to compile drivers for. Only the selected vendor drivers
plus common essentials (llvmpipe, zink, virgl, swrast) are built.

Use multiple entries for multi-GPU setups (e.g. Intel iGPU + NVIDIA dGPU).
An empty list (default) builds all drivers.
name: myModules.hardware.graphics.mesaGit.drivers

description: Whether to enable mesa-git (bleeding-edge) instead of nixpkgs mesa.
name: myModules.hardware.graphics.mesaGit.enable

description:
Gallium drivers to enable in RustiCL (Mesa's OpenCL implementation).
GPU vendor modules append their driver automatically when their openCL
option is enabled. Set by gpu-amd (radeonsi) and gpu-intel (iris).
Assembled into RUSTICL_ENABLE session variable as a comma-separated list.
name: myModules.hardware.graphics.openCL.rusticlDrivers

description:
systemd-resolved DNSOverTLS policy. "true" requires DoT and refuses
plaintext fallback (recommended). "opportunistic" tries DoT first,
falls back to plaintext on failure (weaker guarantee but more
robust on captive portals). "false" disables DoT entirely.
name: myModules.hardware.networking.dnsOverTls

description: Whether to enable Networking configuration.
name: myModules.hardware.networking.enable

description:
DNS nameservers. Default is Mullvad's adblock DoT resolver with
SNI hint so systemd-resolved validates the TLS certificate.
Override per-host if you want a different upstream.
name: myModules.hardware.networking.nameservers

description: Port ranges to open on TCP and UDP (e.g. `[{ from = 1000; to = 2000; }]`)
name: myModules.hardware.networking.openPortRanges

name: myModules.hardware.pipewire.enable

name: myModules.hardware.pipewire.lowLatency

description: Audio buffer size in samples (lower = less latency, more CPU). 256=5.3ms, 512=10.7ms, 1024=21ms
name: myModules.hardware.pipewire.quantum

description: Whether to enable Power management configuration.
name: myModules.hardware.power.enable

description: Whether to enable Laptop power optimizations (TLP).
name: myModules.hardware.power.laptop

name: myModules.hardware.udevAccess.debuggingProbes

name: myModules.hardware.udevAccess.enable

description: Saleae Logic analyzer udev rules
name: myModules.hardware.udevAccess.saleae

description: Whether to enable UPower (battery/power monitoring).
name: myModules.hardware.upower.enable

description: Whether to enable USB device power management fix (prevents WiFi drops).
name: myModules.hardware.usbPower.enable

description: USB vendor ID to disable power management for (default: Realtek).
name: myModules.hardware.usbPower.vendorId

description: Whether to enable USB multiplexing daemon (iOS device support).
name: myModules.hardware.usbmuxd.enable

description:
CPU microarchitecture tier:
  - `v2` — x86-64-v2 (SSE4.2, no AVX2). Ivy Bridge / older AMD.
  - `v3` — x86-64-v3 (AVX2 + BMI2). Haswell+ / Zen+.
  - `v4` — x86-64-v4 (AVX-512). Zen4+ / Sapphire Rapids+.
Consumed by kernel.nix as mArch default (see
`parts/boot/kernel.nix`).
name: myModules.host.tier

description: USB vendor ID for the Ducky keyboard
name: myModules.input.duckyOneXMini.vendor

name: myModules.input.libinput.enable

name: myModules.input.libinput.naturalScrolling

name: myModules.input.libinput.tapping

name: myModules.input.ratbagd.enable

description: Whether to enable StreamController (Elgato Stream Deck).
name: myModules.input.streamcontroller.enable

description: Mouse DPI setting (reported to libinput via HWDB)
name: myModules.input.yeetmouse.devices.g502.dpi

name: myModules.input.yeetmouse.devices.g502.enable

description: USB product ID for the wired G502
name: myModules.input.yeetmouse.devices.g502.wiredProductId

description: USB product ID for the Lightspeed Receiver
name: myModules.input.yeetmouse.devices.g502.wirelessProductId

description: Whether to enable YeetMouse kernel mouse acceleration driver.
name: myModules.input.yeetmouse.enable

description: Apple keyboard hid_apple configuration (fnMode, Option/Command swap)
name: myModules.macbook.hidApple.enable

description: Apple keyboard fn key behavior (0=disabled, 1=press fn for media, 2=press fn for F-keys)
name: myModules.macbook.hidApple.fnMode

description: Swap Option and Command keys (makes Cmd act as Alt)
name: myModules.macbook.hidApple.swapOptCmd

description: mbpfan daemon for MacBook fan control
name: myModules.macbook.mbpfan.enable

description: Temperature for high fan speed (Celsius)
name: myModules.macbook.mbpfan.highTemp

description: Temperature to start ramping fan (Celsius)
name: myModules.macbook.mbpfan.lowTemp

description: Maximum temperature before full fan (Celsius)
name: myModules.macbook.mbpfan.maxTemp

description: Fan polling interval in seconds
name: myModules.macbook.mbpfan.pollingInterval

description: Whether to enable MacBook kernel patches (AppleSMC fixes, AT24 warning suppression).
name: myModules.macbook.patches.enable

description: Whether to enable Broadcom BCM4331 WiFi via b43 driver (MacBook Pro 9,2).
name: myModules.macbook.wifi.enable

description: Whether to enable Nix daemon configuration and settings.
name: myModules.nix.nix.enable

description:
Where nix-daemon gets the GitHub API token (for rate-limited
flake fetches).
  • `gh-cli` — activation script reads `gh auth token` into
    /etc/nix/github-token. Opportunistic (no-op if gh not
    configured for root).
  • `none` — no GitHub token configured. Flake updates hit
    unauthenticated rate limits.
name: myModules.nix.nix.githubTokenSource

description: Whether to enable nix-ld for running unpatched binaries.
name: myModules.nix.nixLd.enable

description: Libraries for nix-ld to provide to unpatched binaries
name: myModules.nix.nixLd.libraries

description: Whether to enable offload builds to a remote nix-daemon over SSH.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.enable

description:
Additional hostnames/IPs the remote answers to. Added to
the SSH known_hosts entry alongside `hostName`, so the
same host public key is trusted no matter which name was
used to connect (hostname + .local + LAN IP, for example).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.extraHostNames

description: Remote builder hostname (SSH-reachable).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.hostName

description:
Remote builder's SSH host public key — get it from the
remote with `cat /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key.pub`.
When set, the client pre-trusts the remote in its root
known_hosts so nix-daemon's non-interactive SSH never
hits a trust-on-first-use prompt. If left null, the
client falls back to StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new
semantics (first connection pins the key).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.hostPublicKey

description:
Parallel derivations the remote can build concurrently.
Rule of thumb: size to remote's logical cores (physical ×
SMT). Each build internally uses `nix.settings.cores`
worker threads, so the product must fit hardware.

Examples:
  • 9950X3D (16C/32T): `maxJobs = 32` with `cores = 1`
    → 32 concurrent compiles, each single-threaded. Best
    for many-small-derivations workloads.
  • 9950X3D balanced: `maxJobs = 16` with `cores = 4`
    → 16 builds × 4 threads = 64 compile-threads. Best
    for kernel/chromium/LibreOffice.
  • Conservative default (8): works on any modern quad.

`nix.buildMachines` takes an integer here — there is no
"auto" for remotes (the remote's own `nix.settings.max-
jobs = "auto"` governs whether it accepts the submission).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.maxJobs

description:
Relative speed vs local. Higher = nix prefers this remote
when the local machine and remote both support a given
build. 10× for ryzen-vs-MBP (roughly: Zen 5 16C/32T
~20× the compile-wall-clock of Ivy Bridge 2C/4T on GCC).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.speedFactor

description: Path to SSH private key on THIS host for remote builder auth.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.sshKey

description: SSH user on the remote builder. Must be in trusted-users there.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.sshUser

description:
LAN IP for the remote. When set, adds a `networking.hosts`
entry so `hostName` resolves even if mDNS breaks. Leave
null to rely entirely on mDNS/DNS resolution.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.staticIp

description: Build features the remote supports (needed for compilers + VM tests).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.supportedFeatures

description: System architecture the remote can build for.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.client.system

description:
SSH public keys of clients allowed to submit builds.
Paste output of `sudo cat /root/.ssh/remotebuild.pub` from each client here.
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.server.authorizedKeys

description: Whether to enable accept remote build requests (adds remotebuild user + trusted).
name: myModules.nix.remoteBuilder.server.enable

description: Primary system username used across all modules
name: myModules.primaryUser

description: Whether to enable agenix secret management.
name: myModules.security.agenix.enable

description: Private identity files agenix may use to decrypt host secrets.
name: myModules.security.agenix.identityPaths

description: Encrypted agenix secrets keyed by runtime secret name. Defaults each entry to `secretsRoot/<name>.age`.
name: myModules.security.agenix.secrets

description: Directory containing encrypted agenix secret files.
name: myModules.security.agenix.secretsRoot

description: Whether to enable System-wide security hardening.
name: myModules.security.hardening.enable

description: Firejail sandboxing
name: myModules.security.hardening.firejail.enable

description: Whether portmaster.service starts automatically on boot. When false, the service is installed but must be started manually with `sudo systemctl start portmaster`.
name: myModules.security.portmaster.autostart

description: Whether to enable Portmaster privacy firewall.
name: myModules.security.portmaster.enable

description: Extra command-line arguments for portmaster-core
name: myModules.security.portmaster.extraArgs

description:
Hard declarative overrides — applied on every preStart, UI edits are
reverted. Use for settings that MUST stay a specific value or the
system breaks (Mullvad-compat DNS flags, kill switch, resolver
listening address).
name: myModules.security.portmaster.forceSettings

description: Whether to enable Portmaster system tray notifier (autostart).
name: myModules.security.portmaster.notifier

description:
Soft declarative settings — seeded into /var/lib/portmaster/config.json
on first boot, then UI edits win. Use for preferences the user may want
to tweak live (filter list selections, expertise level, notifications).
name: myModules.security.portmaster.settings

description:
Whether to enable Mullvad + Portmaster coexistence. Insert an iptables RETURN rule
at the top of PORTMASTER-INGEST-OUTPUT so Portmaster does not
overwrite Mullvad's fwmark on WireGuard-encapsulated packets.
Requires both myModules.security.portmaster.enable and an active
Mullvad daemon — the rule is harmless when Mullvad is stopped
.
name: myModules.security.portmasterMullvadCompat.enable

description:
Mullvad's WireGuard fwmark, as hex. Userspace wireguard-go
stamps this via SO_MARK on every encapsulated UDP packet; the
matching `ip rule` entry uses `fwmark 0x6d6f6c65`. Mullvad has
shipped this value since the linux daemon gained userspace WG
support; changing it here only makes sense if Mullvad upstream
changes the constant.
name: myModules.security.portmasterMullvadCompat.mark

description: Whether to enable Secure SSH server configuration.
name: myModules.security.ssh.enable

description: IP ranges to never ban (add your LAN/VPN subnets)
name: myModules.security.ssh.fail2banIgnoreIPs

description: List of trusted SSH public keys
name: myModules.security.ssh.trustedKeys

description: Whether to enable x86 MSR access (APERF/MPERF, RAPL energy counters).
name: myModules.sensors.msr.enable

description: Whether to enable Nuvoton NCT67xx Super I/O sensors (motherboard Vcore, fans, temps).
name: myModules.sensors.nct6775.enable

description: Whether to enable AMD ryzen_smu kernel module (Curve Optimizer, PBO, boost override).
name: myModules.sensors.ryzenSmu.enable

description: Whether to enable Zenpower5 AMD CPU sensors (replaces k10temp — Zen 1 through Zen 5).
name: myModules.sensors.zenpower.enable

description: Whether to enable Avahi mDNS/zeroconf daemon + NSS integration.
name: myModules.services.avahi.enable

description:
Open UDP port 5353 for mDNS multicast in the host firewall.
Required for any mDNS interaction — both advertisement AND
consumption rely on inbound multicast. Disabling this blocks
other hosts' announcements too. To stop advertising while
staying able to resolve `.local` names, set `publish = false`
and keep `openFirewall = true`.
name: myModules.services.avahi.openFirewall

description:
Whether this host advertises itself via mDNS. Default true
so other LAN hosts can resolve us by `.local` name. Set
false on hosts that shouldn't be discoverable.
name: myModules.services.avahi.publish

description:
Run cups-browsed to auto-discover network printers.
Set false on hosts with no printers to silence periodic
Create-Printer-Subscriptions client-error-bad-request
churn in cupsd logs (cups-browsed renews stale leases
every few hours; with no targets the first Create probe
carries a short-form payload cupsd rejects before the
retry with full attrs succeeds).
name: myModules.services.cups.browsing

description: Whether to enable Printing support (CUPS).
name: myModules.services.cups.enable

description: Regex matching process names earlyoom must never kill.
name: myModules.services.earlyoom.avoidRegex

description: Whether to enable Early OOM killer (prevents system freezes).
name: myModules.services.earlyoom.enable

description: Minimum free memory percentage before killing
name: myModules.services.earlyoom.memoryThreshold

description:
Regex matching process names that earlyoom should preferentially kill
under memory pressure. Per-host overridable so low-RAM hosts (e.g.
MBP 9,2) can include nix-daemon, build compilers, and editors that
commonly trigger OOM cascades.
name: myModules.services.earlyoom.preferRegex

description: Minimum free swap percentage before killing
name: myModules.services.earlyoom.swapThreshold

description: Whether to enable GeoClue2 location service.
name: myModules.services.geoclue.enable

description: Whether to enable Mullvad VPN — declarative daemon settings.
name: myModules.services.mullvad.enable

description:
Forwarded to `services.mullvad-vpn-declarative.settings`.
See Daaboulex/mullvad-vpn-nix README for option reference.
name: myModules.services.mullvad.settings

description: GPU render node for hardware encoding.
name: myModules.services.sunshine.adapterName

description: Applications exposed to Moonlight (services.sunshine.applications).
name: myModules.services.sunshine.applications

description: PipeWire sink to capture. Null = default monitor. Set to .monitor of your speakers to stream WHILE audio plays on desktop.
name: myModules.services.sunshine.audioSink

description: Whether to enable Sunshine game streaming server (Moonlight-compatible).
name: myModules.services.sunshine.enable

description: Hardware encoder. AMD on Linux uses vaapi (Mesa).
name: myModules.services.sunshine.encoder

description: Additional Sunshine settings merged into services.sunshine.settings.
name: myModules.services.sunshine.extraSettings

description: Display index to capture (Wayland uses numeric index, check sunshine.log for 'Found display: <N>').
name: myModules.services.sunshine.outputName

description: If true, audio plays on desktop speakers AND streams to Moonlight (clone). If false, only streams (silent locally).
name: myModules.services.sunshine.streamAudioToClientAndHost

description: Virtual sink name — creates a separate sink so audio ONLY goes to stream, silent on desktop. Set to null to mirror to desktop instead.
name: myModules.services.sunshine.virtualSink

description: Peer devices to sync with
name: myModules.services.syncthing.devices

description: Whether to enable Syncthing continuous file synchronization.
name: myModules.services.syncthing.enable

description: Folders to sync
name: myModules.services.syncthing.folders

description:
Seconds to delay the syncthing daemon after login. On slow SSDs
(DRAM-less, SATA), the initial full-folder scan after boot can
saturate the I/O queue and freeze the UI. A delay lets the desktop
session settle first. 0 = start immediately (default).
name: myModules.services.syncthing.startDelay

description: Whether to enable btrbk incremental btrfs snapshot replication.
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.enable

description:
Retention on the SOURCE side (how many short-lived snapshots
kept live on the root filesystem while btrbk runs). Syntax:
"24h 14d 8w 6m" = hourly × 24, daily × 14, weekly × 8, monthly × 6.
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.snapshotPreserve

description:
Mount point of the source btrfs filesystem. Usually "/".
btrbk will snapshot subvolumes accessible from here.
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.sourcePath

description:
Subvolume names to replicate. Default covers root + home +
nix store + logs. Skip @cache / @tmp / @snapshots (regenerable
or meta).
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.subvolumes

description:
Mount point of the target btrfs filesystem on the second drive.
Must be a btrfs filesystem. Snapshots land in this directory.
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.targetPath

description:
Retention on the TARGET drive. Longer history here — target
exists precisely to hold deep backup history.
name: myModules.storage.btrbk.targetPreserve
myModules.storage.btrbk.timer

description: Whether to enable Universal filesystem support.
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enable

description:
Master switch — when true, every category is enabled
regardless of the per-category `enable<X>` toggles. The
per-category toggles are an OR-gate, not an override:
`enableAll = false; enableLinux = true;` still enables
Linux. To disable a category, set `enableAll = false` AND
the per-category toggle false.
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableAll

description: Legacy filesystems (ReiserFS)
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableLegacy

description: Linux filesystems (ext4, btrfs, xfs, f2fs)
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableLinux

description: macOS filesystems (HFS+)
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableMac

description: Optical disc filesystems (ISO 9660, UDF)
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableOptical

description: Windows filesystems (NTFS, exFAT, FAT32)
name: myModules.storage.filesystems.enableWindows

description: Whether to enable Periodic SSD TRIM.
name: myModules.storage.fstrim.enable

description: How often to run fstrim
name: myModules.storage.fstrim.interval

description: Whether to enable CachyOS amdgpuGcnCompat.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.amdgpuGcnCompat.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS audio.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.audio.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS coredump.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.coredump.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS debuginfod.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.debuginfod.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS system optimizations (upstream-matched settings).
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS ioSchedulers.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.ioSchedulers.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS networkManager.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.networkManager.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS ntsync.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.ntsync.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS nvidia.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.nvidia.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS storage.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.storage.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS systemd.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.systemd.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS thp.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.thp.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS timesyncd.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.timesyncd.enable

description: Whether to enable CachyOS zram.
name: myModules.tuning.cachyos.zram.enable

description: Whether to grant primaryUser access to MSR devices and SMU sysfs via a dedicated group and udev rules. No sudo required for monitoring and CO access.
name: myModules.tuning.corecycler.deviceAccess

description: Whether to enable CoreCyclerLx device access (MSR, SMU sysfs, dmesg).
name: myModules.tuning.corecycler.enable

description: Ananicy-cpp process prioritization
name: myModules.tuning.performance.ananicy

description: Whether to enable Performance tuning and optimization.
name: myModules.tuning.performance.enable

description:
CPU frequency governor (cpufreq scaling_governor). Finite set
defined by the kernel cpufreq subsystem. `powersave` is the
safe default on scx-scheduled hosts (scx handles frequency
decisions); pick `performance` for VFIO or low-latency hosts.
name: myModules.tuning.performance.governor

description: IRQ balancing across CPU cores
name: myModules.tuning.performance.irqbalance

description: Whether to enable Sched-ext (scx) userspace CPU schedulers.
name: myModules.tuning.performance.scx.enable

description: Extra arguments passed to the scheduler
name: myModules.tuning.performance.scx.extraArgs

description: Which SCX scheduler to run
name: myModules.tuning.performance.scx.scheduler

description: Whether to enable Extra performance sysctls: BBR, CAKE, tcp_fastopen, buffer sizes, max_map_count.
name: myModules.tuning.sysctls.enable

description: Whether to enable User configuration.
name: myModules.users.enable

description: Override CachyOS ananicy-cpp rules for QEMU. Default rules classify QEMU as Heavy_CPU (nice=9, ionice=7) which deprioritizes VM performance. This adds custom rules that give QEMU and libvirt high priority instead.
name: myModules.vfio.anancyOverride

description: static = vfio-pci.ids kernel param (GPU always captured at boot); dynamic = libvirt hooks bind/unbind on VM start/stop
name: myModules.vfio.bindMethod

description: Whether to enable VFIO GPU passthrough with stealth VM management.
name: myModules.vfio.enable

description: Evdev input passthrough for keyboard/mouse
name: myModules.vfio.evdev.enable

description: Path to keyboard event device (e.g. /dev/input/by-id/...)
name: myModules.vfio.evdev.keyboardPath

description: Path to mouse event device (e.g. /dev/input/by-id/...)
name: myModules.vfio.evdev.mousePath

description: Hex bitmask of host-only CPUs (CCD1 cores). Used to restrict scx scheduler during VM runtime. Example: 0xff00ff00 for cores 8-15 + threads 24-31 on a dual-CCD Zen 5.
name: myModules.vfio.hostCpuMask

description: Host GPU driver used for recovery rebinding when a passthrough device is stuck on vfio-pci from a prior run. Set to 'nouveau' or 'nvidia' for NVIDIA hosts, 'i915' for Intel. Defaults to 'amdgpu'.
name: myModules.vfio.hostGpuDriver

description: Number of hugepages to allocate
name: myModules.vfio.hugepages.count

description: Hugepage allocation for VM memory (allocated on VM start, freed on VM stop)
name: myModules.vfio.hugepages.enable

description: Hugepage size (1G = fewer TLB misses, best for gaming VMs)
name: myModules.vfio.hugepages.size

description: KVMFR shared memory for Looking Glass frame relay
name: myModules.vfio.kvmfr.enable

description: KVMFR shared memory size in MB (32=1440p SDR, 64=4K SDR, 128=4K HDR)
name: myModules.vfio.kvmfr.memoryMB

description: QEMU machine type for VM definitions
name: myModules.vfio.machineType

description: Restrict the scx scheduler to host-only cores while a VM is running. When true, the hook restarts scx_bpfland with --primary-domain set to hostCpuMask (NOT stopped — keeps scheduling benefits on host cores). Restored to full CPU set on VM stop.
name: myModules.vfio.restrictScxToHost

description:
DRM device paths for KWIN_DRM_DEVICES. First device is primary render GPU —
set iGPU first for safe GPU passthrough. null = KWin auto-detects (unsafe).

WARNING: KWIN_DRM_DEVICES uses ':' as separator. Do NOT use /dev/dri/by-path/
paths — they contain ':' in the PCI address and will be split into garbage,
causing KWin to fail with "No suitable DRM devices have been found" and
trigger an SDDM infinite-login loop. Use /dev/dri/cardN instead.
name: myModules.vfio.sessionGpuDevices

description: PCI vendor:device IDs for static vfio-pci binding (e.g. ["1002:7550" "1002:ab40"])
name: myModules.vfio.staticPciIds

default:
"  echo "=== BetterTiming: TSC compensation patch ==="

  # ---------- 1. Add tracking fields to struct kvm_vcpu ----------
  # Insert last_exit_start and total_exit_time after valid_wakeup
  if grep -q 'bool valid_wakeup;' include/linux/kvm_host.h; then
    sed -i '/bool valid_wakeup;/a\\n\tu64 last_exit_start;\n\tu64 total_exit_time;' \
      include/linux/kvm_host.h
    echo "[OK] kvm_host.h: added timing fields to struct kvm_vcpu"
  else
    echo "[FAIL] kvm_host.h: could not find valid_wakeup field"
    exit 1
  fi

  # ---------- 2. Rename vcpu_enter_guest → vcpu_enter_guest_real ----------
  if grep -q '^static int vcpu_enter_guest(struct kvm_vcpu \*vcpu)' arch/x86/kvm/x86.c; then
    sed -i 's/^static int vcpu_enter_guest(struct kvm_vcpu \*vcpu)/static int vcpu_enter_guest_real(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)/' \
      arch/x86/kvm/x86.c
    echo "[OK] x86.c: renamed vcpu_enter_guest to vcpu_enter_guest_real"
  else
    echo "[FAIL] x86.c: could not find vcpu_enter_guest definition"
    exit 1
  fi

  # ---------- 3. Add wrapper vcpu_enter_guest after vcpu_enter_guest_real ----------
  # Insert after the closing brace of vcpu_enter_guest_real, which is the first
  # solo '}' on a line after the function. We find it by locating the next function
  # (kvm_vcpu_running) and inserting before it.
  if grep -q '^static bool kvm_vcpu_running(struct kvm_vcpu \*vcpu)' arch/x86/kvm/x86.c; then
    sed -i '/^static bool kvm_vcpu_running(struct kvm_vcpu \*vcpu)/i\
static int vcpu_enter_guest(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tint result;\
\tu64 difference;\
\
\tvcpu->last_exit_start = rdtsc();\
\
\tresult = vcpu_enter_guest_real(vcpu);\
\
\tif (vcpu->run->exit_reason == 123) {\
\t\tdifference = rdtsc() - vcpu->last_exit_start;\
\t\tvcpu->total_exit_time += difference;\
\t}\
\
\treturn result;\
}\
' arch/x86/kvm/x86.c
    echo "[OK] x86.c: added vcpu_enter_guest wrapper"
  else
    echo "[FAIL] x86.c: could not find kvm_vcpu_running to insert wrapper before"
    exit 1
  fi

  # ---------- 4. Patch MSR_IA32_TSC read to return compensated time ----------
  # Replace the TSC computation block in kvm_get_msr_common.
  # In 6.19, the MSR_IA32_TSC case has a block starting with:
  #   u64 offset, ratio;
  # We replace the entire case body with our compensated read.
  if grep -q 'case MSR_IA32_TSC: {' arch/x86/kvm/x86.c; then
    # Use awk for multi-line replacement within the MSR_IA32_TSC case
    awk '
    /case MSR_IA32_TSC: \{/ {
      print "\tcase MSR_IA32_TSC: {"
      print "\t\tu64 bt_diff;"
      print "\t\tu64 bt_total;"
      print ""
      print "\t\tbt_diff = rdtsc() - vcpu->last_exit_start;"
      print "\t\tbt_total = vcpu->total_exit_time + bt_diff;"
      print ""
      print "\t\tmsr_info->data = rdtsc() - bt_total;"
      print ""
      print "\t\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;"
      print "\t\tbreak;"
      print "\t}"
      # Skip old content until the closing brace+break
      in_tsc = 1
      next
    }
    in_tsc && /^\tcase / { in_tsc = 0; print; next }
    in_tsc && /^\t\}/ { in_tsc = 0; next }
    in_tsc { next }
    { print }
    ' arch/x86/kvm/x86.c > arch/x86/kvm/x86.c.tmp && \
      mv arch/x86/kvm/x86.c.tmp arch/x86/kvm/x86.c
    echo "[OK] x86.c: patched MSR_IA32_TSC to return compensated time"
  else
    echo "[WARN] x86.c: could not find MSR_IA32_TSC case block — skipping"
  fi

  # ---------- 5. Enable RDTSC interception in init_vmcb ----------
  if grep -q 'svm_set_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RSM);' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
    sed -i '/svm_set_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RSM);/a\\tsvm_set_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RDTSC);' \
      arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
    echo "[OK] svm.c: enabled RDTSC interception in init_vmcb"
  else
    echo "[WARN] svm.c: could not find INTERCEPT_RSM anchor for RDTSC interception"
  fi

  # ---------- 6. Add handle_rdtsc_interception handler ----------
  # Insert before the svm_exit_handlers table definition.
  # In 6.19, handlers take (struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu), and we use to_svm() to get svm.
  if grep -q '^static int (\*const svm_exit_handlers\[\])' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
    sed -i '/^static int (\*const svm_exit_handlers\[\])/i\
static int handle_rdtsc_interception(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tstruct vcpu_svm *svm = to_svm(vcpu);\
\tu64 difference;\
\tu64 final_time;\
\tu64 data;\
\
\tdifference = rdtsc() - vcpu->last_exit_start;\
\tfinal_time = vcpu->total_exit_time + difference;\
\
\tdata = rdtsc() - final_time;\
\
\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RAX] = data & -1u;\
\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RDX] = (data >> 32) & -1u;\
\
\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;\
\n\treturn kvm_skip_emulated_instruction(vcpu);\
}\
' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
    echo "[OK] svm.c: added handle_rdtsc_interception handler"
  else
    echo "[FAIL] svm.c: could not find svm_exit_handlers table"
    exit 1
  fi

  # ---------- 7. Register RDTSC handler in svm_exit_handlers table ----------
  # Add [SVM_EXIT_RDTSC] entry after [SVM_EXIT_AVIC_UNACCELERATED_ACCESS]
  if grep -q 'SVM_EXIT_AVIC_UNACCELERATED_ACCESS.*avic_unaccelerated_access_interception' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
    sed -i '/\[SVM_EXIT_AVIC_UNACCELERATED_ACCESS\].*=.*avic_unaccelerated_access_interception/a\\t[SVM_EXIT_RDTSC]\t\t\t\t= handle_rdtsc_interception,' \
      arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
    echo "[OK] svm.c: registered SVM_EXIT_RDTSC handler"
  else
    echo "[WARN] svm.c: could not find AVIC_UNACCELERATED_ACCESS entry for RDTSC registration"
  fi

  # ---------- 8. Tag exit_reason=123 on CPUID, WBINVD, XSETBV, INVD ----------
  # In 6.19, these map directly to kvm_emulate_* functions. We create wrapper
  # functions that set exit_reason=123 then call through, and update the table.

  # 8a. Create wrapper functions (insert before svm_exit_handlers table)
  sed -i '/^static int handle_rdtsc_interception/i\
static int stealth_cpuid_interception(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;\
\treturn kvm_emulate_cpuid(vcpu);\
}\
\
static int stealth_wbinvd_interception(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;\
\treturn kvm_emulate_wbinvd(vcpu);\
}\
\
static int stealth_xsetbv_interception(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;\
\treturn kvm_emulate_xsetbv(vcpu);\
}\
\
static int stealth_invd_interception(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu)\
{\
\tvcpu->run->exit_reason = 123;\
\treturn kvm_emulate_invd(vcpu);\
}\
' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  echo "[OK] svm.c: created stealth wrapper functions for exit_reason tagging"

  # 8b. Replace handler table entries to use wrappers
  sed -i 's/\[SVM_EXIT_CPUID\].*=.*kvm_emulate_cpuid,/[SVM_EXIT_CPUID]\t\t\t= stealth_cpuid_interception,/' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  sed -i 's/\[SVM_EXIT_WBINVD\].*=.*kvm_emulate_wbinvd,/[SVM_EXIT_WBINVD]\t\t\t= stealth_wbinvd_interception,/' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  sed -i 's/\[SVM_EXIT_XSETBV\].*=.*kvm_emulate_xsetbv,/[SVM_EXIT_XSETBV]\t\t\t= stealth_xsetbv_interception,/' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  sed -i 's/\[SVM_EXIT_INVD\].*=.*kvm_emulate_invd,/[SVM_EXIT_INVD]\t\t\t\t= stealth_invd_interception,/' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  echo "[OK] svm.c: updated exit handler table to use stealth wrappers"

  echo "=== BetterTiming: patch complete ==="
echo "=== CPUID Spoofing: Hypervisor-Phantom style patch ==="

# ---------- 1. Add reenter_guest_fast label before svm_vcpu_enter_exit ----------
# The call site in svm_vcpu_run looks like:
#   svm_vcpu_enter_exit(vcpu, spec_ctrl_intercepted);
# We add a label before it so we can goto from the CPUID handler.
if grep -q 'svm_vcpu_enter_exit(vcpu, spec_ctrl_intercepted);' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
  sed -i '/svm_vcpu_enter_exit(vcpu, spec_ctrl_intercepted);/i\\nreenter_guest_fast:' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  echo "[OK] svm.c: added reenter_guest_fast label"
else
  echo "[FAIL] svm.c: could not find svm_vcpu_enter_exit call site"
  exit 1
fi

# ---------- 2. Add CPUID leaf 0 spoofing after svm_vcpu_enter_exit ----------
# Insert the interception block between svm_vcpu_enter_exit() and the
# spec_ctrl_restore_host call. We anchor on the line after the enter_exit call.
#
# The code checks if exit_code == SVM_EXIT_CPUID and leaf (RAX) == 0.
# If so, it spoofs AuthenticAMD vendor string, caps max leaf at 0x16,
# advances RIP, and jumps back to re-enter the guest.
if grep -q 'svm_vcpu_enter_exit(vcpu, spec_ctrl_intercepted);' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
  sed -i '/^\tsvm_vcpu_enter_exit(vcpu, spec_ctrl_intercepted);$/a\\n\t/*\n\t * CPUID leaf 0 spoofing — Hypervisor-Phantom technique.\n\t * Intercept at lowest level, spoof vendor, re-enter without full exit.\n\t * Required kernel cmdline: mitigations=off idle=poll processor.max_cstate=1 tsc=reliable\n\t */\n\tif (unlikely(svm->vmcb->control.exit_code == SVM_EXIT_CPUID)) {\n\t\tif (svm->vmcb->save.rax == 0) {\n\t\t\tsvm->vmcb->save.rax = 0x16;\n\n\t\t\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RBX] = 0x68747541; /* Auth */\n\t\t\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RCX] = 0x444d4163; /* cAMD */\n\t\t\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RDX] = 0x69746e65; /* enti */\n\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tu64 next_rip = svm->vmcb->control.next_rip;\n\t\t\t\tif (!next_rip)\n\t\t\t\t\tnext_rip = svm->vmcb->save.rip + svm->vmcb->control.insn_len;\n\t\t\t\tsvm->vmcb->save.rip = next_rip;\n\t\t\t\tvcpu->arch.regs[VCPU_REGS_RIP] = next_rip;\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tgoto reenter_guest_fast;\n\t\t}\n\t}' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  echo "[OK] svm.c: added CPUID leaf 0 spoofing with reenter_guest_fast"
else
  echo "[FAIL] svm.c: could not find svm_vcpu_enter_exit for CPUID insertion"
  exit 1
fi

# ---------- 3. Clear RDTSC/RDTSCP interception in init_vmcb ----------
# This ensures the guest can read TSC directly. When BetterTiming is also
# applied, its INTERCEPT_RDTSC set call (added after INTERCEPT_RSM) overrides
# this clear since it runs later in init_vmcb.
if grep -q 'svm_set_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RSM);' arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c; then
  sed -i '/svm_set_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RSM);/a\\tsvm_clr_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RDTSC);\n\tsvm_clr_intercept(svm, INTERCEPT_RDTSCP);' \
    arch/x86/kvm/svm/svm.c
  echo "[OK] svm.c: cleared RDTSC/RDTSCP interception in init_vmcb"
else
  echo "[WARN] svm.c: could not find INTERCEPT_RSM anchor for clearing RDTSC"
fi

echo "=== CPUID Spoofing: patch complete ==="
"
description: Combined kernel postPatch script. Apply via kernel overrideAttrs in host config.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth._kernelPostPatch

description: 6-char ACPI OEM ID (padded with spaces). Defeats ACPI table header OEM ID checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.acpiOem.id

description: 8-char ACPI OEM Table ID (padded with spaces). Defeats ACPI table header OEM Table ID checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.acpiOem.tableId

description: Include a fake ACPI battery device in the SSDT table. Defeats 'no battery = server/VM' heuristic.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.acpiSsdt.fakeBattery

description: Include CPU and VRM thermal zone probes (defeats MSAcpi_ThermalZoneTemperature WMI detection).
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.acpiSsdt.sensorProbes

description: Include spoofed ACPI device entries in the SSDT table. Defeats ACPI namespace enumeration checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.acpiSsdt.spoofedDevices

description: Pass through IA32_APERF/MPERF MSRs to guest (defeats IET-based VM detection). Requires kernel 6.18+.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.aperfMperf

description: CPU current speed in MHz for SMBIOS type 4. null = omit.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.cpuIdentity.currentSpeed

description: CPU max speed in MHz for SMBIOS type 4. null = omit.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.cpuIdentity.maxSpeed

description: CPU model string reported to guest (e.g. 'AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor'). null = use host CPU.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.cpuIdentity.modelId

description: CPUID leaf 0 spoofing via Hypervisor-Phantom technique. Defeats anti-cheat that checks the hypervisor-present CPUID bit (leaf 1, bit 31) and hypervisor vendor string (leaf 0x40000000).
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.cpuidSpoof.enable

description: Emulated disk model string (24 chars padded with spaces). Defeats Win32_DiskDrive.Model checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.disk.model

description: Emulated optical drive model (24 chars padded). Defeats Win32_CDROMDrive.Name checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.disk.opticalModel

description: Monitor DPI.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.dpi

description: 3-char EDID manufacturer code.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.manufacturer

description: 16-char padded EDID model string.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.model

description: 8-char padded EDID model abbreviation.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.modelAbbrev

description: EDID product code (hex).
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.productCode

description: Monitor serial string.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.serial

description: EDID manufacture week.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.week

description: EDID manufacture year.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.edid.year

description: Whether to enable VFIO stealth anti-detection stack.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.enable

description: Hyper-V vendor_id reported to guest (1–12 chars, libvirt hard limit). Avoid well-known VM values like 'AMDisbetter!' or 'Microsoft Hv'.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.hypervVendorId

description: processor.max_cstate value passed on the kernel command line. Lower values reduce timing jitter that can reveal virtualization.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.kernelParams.maxCState

description: Pass tsc=reliable on the kernel command line. Prevents kernel from falling back to HPET/ACPI PM timer, which anti-cheat can detect.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.kernelParams.tscReliable

description: OUI prefix used when spoofMac is enabled (colon-separated hex, e.g. 00:1b:21). Intel OUI by default.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.macPrefix

description: SMBIOS BIOS vendor string. Defeats WMI Win32_BIOS.Manufacturer checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.biosVendor

description: SMBIOS BIOS version string. Defeats WMI Win32_BIOS.SMBIOSBIOSVersion checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.biosVersion

description: L1 cache size in KB for SMBIOS type 7.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.cache.l1

description: L2 cache size in KB for SMBIOS type 7.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.cache.l2

description: L3 cache size in KB for SMBIOS type 7.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.cache.l3

description: SMBIOS system manufacturer string. Defeats WMI Win32_ComputerSystem.Manufacturer checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.manufacturer

description: Number of DIMMs to report.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.memory.count

description: DIMM manufacturer for SMBIOS type 17. Defeats WMI Win32_PhysicalMemory.Manufacturer checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.memory.manufacturer

description: DIMM part number. Defeats WMI Win32_PhysicalMemory.PartNumber checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.memory.partNumber

description: DIMM size in MB (per module).
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.memory.size

description: Memory speed in MT/s. Defeats WMI Win32_PhysicalMemory.Speed checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.memory.speed

description: SMBIOS product name string. Defeats WMI Win32_ComputerSystem.Model checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.product

description: SMBIOS system serial number. Defeats WMI Win32_ComputerSystemProduct.IdentifyingNumber checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.serial

description: SMBIOS processor socket designator prefix (type 4). Defeats WMI Win32_Processor.SocketDesignation checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.smbios.socketPrefix

description: Spoof guest NIC MAC address with a realistic OUI prefix. Defeats MAC OUI-based VM detection.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.spoofMac

description: Remove VirtIO balloon, RNG, and tablet devices from VM config when stealth is enabled. Defeats PCI device enumeration checks.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.stripVirtio

description: BetterTiming TSC compensation (hides VM exit timing from guests). Defeats timing-based VM detection that measures RDTSC deltas across CPUID/VMCALL.
name: myModules.vfio.stealth.timing.enable

description: Directory for VM disk images and state files
name: myModules.vfio.vmDiskPath

description: Per-VM definitions
name: myModules.vfio.vms
